prompt_authoring.overview: Generate external LLM prompts that preserve IdeaMark Core v1.2.0 YAML validity, structural reference integrity, and projection-driven interpretation for reusable knowledge capture.

recommended_yaml_units: top-level perspectives that record the projection used for extraction
payload_requirements: Each payload must include body, ref, or cache.

required: Occurrence entity values must resolve to entity IDs.
Section occurrences must resolve to occurrence IDs.
Relation endpoints may target entity or section refs.
Use payload.ref.uri for explicit external references.
Keep citation and bibliography data source-backed.
Use payload body, ref, or cache according to available source fidelity.
When a projection is supplied, record it under top-level perspectives using a stable perspective ID.
Use the projection to decide which source details become reusable entities and which details are ignored.
If a section or entity is strongly projection-dependent, attach the perspective ID through the supported perspectives field when practical.
If a template adds fields, keep base payload and local reference rules intact.
Record template usage in header metadata when relevant.

system: You are generating IdeaMark Core v1.2.0 documents.
YAML validity and resolvable local references are mandatory.
Projection-driven interpretation is mandatory when a projection is supplied.
task: Read the projection before reading the source text.
Produce a valid header and top-level namespaces.
Ensure each entity has payload content.
Ensure each occurrence points to an existing entity.
Ensure each section lists existing occurrences.
quality_gate: Must not contain unresolved local references.
Must reflect the supplied projection in extraction choices.
